abstract: An atom that describes the type and ID of a node, as well as other information about the node.
overview:
## Overview

A node header atom is a leaf atom that describes the type and ID of a node, as well as other information about the node. Its atom type is `kQTVRNodeHeaderAtomType` (`'ndhd'`).

The structure of a node header atom is defined by the QTVRNodeHeaderAtom data type:

items.description: The major version number of the file format.
items.name: majorVersion

items.description: The minor version number of the file format.
items.name: minorVersion

items.description: The ID of the string atom that contains the name of the node.
items.name: nameAtomID

items.description: The ID of the string atom that contains a comment for the node.
items.name: commentAtomID

items.description: An atom that is the parent for all hot spot atoms for the node.
items.name: Hot spot parent atom ('hspa')

items.description: An atom that contains general information about a hot spot.
items.name: Hot spot information atom ('hsin')

items.description: An atom that identified the hot spot type.
items.name: Specific information atom

items.description: An atom that specifies information for a link hot spot.
items.name: Link hot spot atom ('link')

title: Node header atom ('ndhd')
